rulings.Case Title: MAYA BLYTH VS. LEAL UGRIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For A Protective Order To Control The Sequence And Timing Of Depositions And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2026, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MAYA BLYTH's Motion For A Protective Order To Control The Sequence And Timing Of Depositions And Request For Monetary Sanctions. 4 - Continued to April 24, 2026 per the parties' agreement. No later than April 20, 2026 counsel for Blyth is required to send an email to Contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to counsel for Ugrin confirming that the parties engaged in a meaningful meet and confer on or after March 18, 2026 on all remaining unresolved issues and identifying, without argument, any still remaining unresolved issues that need to be decided by the court. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. = The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK).

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2026, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ELECTRIC OWL STUDIO, INC.'s Motion To Correct Dismissal. Plaintiff Electric Owl Studio, Inc.'s motion to correct dismissal is granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. The dismissal of this case filed September 29, 2023 is corrected to be a dismissal without prejudice. rulings.Case Title: A2 ACADEMY LLC ET AL VS. SWORD HEALTH, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2026, Line 6, PLAINTIFF A2 ACADEMY LLC, KATHRYN THOMAS, STEPHEN JOHNSTON, ARNOLD WHITMAN's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint. Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file their proposed third amended complaint is denied. The proposed third amended complaint alleges in paragraph 58 the exact language that had been alleged in paragraph 55 of the second amended complaint which was stricken by the order filed July 10, 2025. Plaintiffs' moving papers do not acknowledge the July 10, 2025 order, much less explain why there is good cause to reconsider or vacate the portion of the order striking paragraph 55 in the second amended complaint. Nor does the court discern any basis to reconsider or vacate that portion of the July 10, 2025 order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Demand For Sanctions; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2026, Line 8, PLAINTIFF TEMUR JUMANOV's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Demand For Sanctions. Continued to April 22, 2026 to give the parties a full opportunity to engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts. No later than the end of the day on April 17, 2026 the parties shall meet in person or by remote video platform to meaningfully discuss each unresolved issue raised by this motion. No later than 4pm on April 20, 2026 counsel for Jumanov shall send an email to Contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to counsel for Volkswagen identifying, without argument, any remaining unresolved issues that need to be decided by the court. For guidance of the parties, all documents relating to Jumanov's vehicle are presumptively discoverable and all documents relating to other vehicles of the same make and model are presumptively discoverable only if they are readily available to Volkswagen without need to review or analyze documents in a specific car owner's files or the repair history of a specific vehicle. rulings.Case Title: J. DERYCKX VS. DECKER ELECTRIC CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2026, Line 9, DEFENDANT DECKER ELECTRIC CO., INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Decker Electric Co.'s motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff J. Deryckx is denied. Deryckx has alleged sufficient ultimate facts to show both malice and oppression as those terms are defined in Civil Code 3294(c) (1) and (2). The allegations that Decker terminated Deryckx's employment to avoid the costs of Deryckx's required cancer treatment with the knowledge that depriving Deryckx of his health benefits would deprive Deryckx of the life-sustaining treatment is "despicable conduct": 1) "carried on ...with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of" Deryckx and 2) that subjects Deryckx "to cruel and unjust hardship in conscious disregard of" Deryckx's rights. The court did not address Decker's argument that Deryckx did not adequately allege managing agent liability since that argument was made for the first time in the reply memorandum. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 13, 2026, Line 12, DEFENDANT 450 HAYES VALLEY LLC, BACK OF THE HOUSE, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration. Defendants 450 Hayes Valley LLC and Back of the House, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration is granted. Plaintiff Kiara Maple filed a statement that she agrees to arbitration per the arbitration agreement. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK).
